# dedaub-monitoring

> Command-line interface (CLI) for the Dedaub monitoring platform. Write SQL (DedaubQL) queries against on-chain data on Ethereum and EVM-compatible chains, test them, and deploy real-time blockchain alerts from your terminal. Package name: `monitoring-cli`. Installed command: `dedaub-monitoring`. License: MIT.

## What it does

- Query on-chain transactions, logs, and contracts with SQL (DedaubQL, a SQL dialect with macros for incremental, time-bounded execution).
- Deploy real-time alerts with email notifications for events such as large transfers, fund drains, liquidations, oracle price deviations, and privileged admin/owner actions.
- Manage query folders, schemas, run configs, execution logs, and fired-alert history without leaving the terminal.
- Works across Ethereum, Arbitrum, Base, and other EVM-compatible chains supported by the Dedaub platform (selected via the `--network` option and DedaubQL macros).
- Optional Claude Code skill (`dedaub-monitoring install-skill`, then `/monitoring-cli`) provides an AI-guided alert builder.

## Use cases

- Blockchain monitoring and on-chain alerting
- Smart-contract monitoring and DeFi protocol monitoring
- Security monitoring (drains, rug pulls, oracle manipulation, admin-key actions)
- On-chain data analysis and exploration

## Key commands

- `login` — authenticate via browser OAuth2 device flow
- `tree` — show your query file tree
- `create-folder` / `create-query` — create folders and queries
- `write-query` / `read-query` — set or print a query's SQL
- `run-query` — execute a query and print results
- `get-schema` — show available tables, columns, and macros
- `enable-alerts` / `disable-alerts` / `list-alerts` — manage alert notifications
- `get-logs` / `get-alerts` — inspect execution logs and fired alerts
- `install-skill` — install the Claude Code skill

## Links

- Source and documentation: https://github.com/Dedaub/monitoring-cli
- Dedaub monitoring platform: https://app.dedaub.com
- DedaubQL API reference: https://docs.dedaub.com/docs/monitoring/TSQLApiReference/
